data.title: Bittensor Experiences Significant Market Value Loss Following Covenant AI Departure
data.description: Bittensor's market value dropped by nearly $900 million after Covenant AI announced its departure from the network. According to NS3.AI, CryptoSlate data revealed that TAO, Bittensor's native token, fell 27% from $338 to $285 within approximately two hours before slightly recovering to $294. CoinGlass data indicated that the event led to $11 million in long liquidations. The split has highlighted a governance dispute between Covenant AI founder Sam Dare and Bittensor co-founder Jacob Steeves.

data.title: WLD tokens will see a reduction in their unlocking rate in July, with an overall decrease of 43%.
data.description: The official World blog announced that the WLD token unlocking rate will be automatically reduced by approximately 43% starting July 24, 2026. Currently, 4.9 billion WLD tokens (49% of the total supply) have been unlocked, with approximately 3.3 billion in circulation. Specifically, the unlocking rate for the World community will decrease from 3.2 million tokens per day to 1.6 million tokens per day, a 50% reduction; the unlocking rate for the team and investors will decrease from 1.9 million tokens per day to 1.3 million tokens per day, a 32% reduction. The overall unlocking amount will decrease from approximately 5.1 million tokens per day to approximately 2.9 million tokens per day. WLD uses a continuous linear unlocking mechanism, with no one-time large-scale unlocking (cliff). This rate reduction stems from established on-chain contract arrangements aimed at gradually reducing selling pressure.

data.title: Circle unveils its vision for an internet finance system: advancing cross-chain interoperability, liquidity coordination, and institutional-grade asset issuance.
data.description: Circle officially released its 2026 vision for the development of its internet finance system, proposing to focus on building cross-chain interoperability infrastructure, liquidity orchestration capabilities, and an institutional-grade asset issuance system to promote the efficient flow of value within a multi-chain ecosystem. Regarding cross-chain infrastructure, Circle stated that it will launch features such as "Fast Transfer" in the future, enabling second-level settlement without waiting for final confirmation from the source chain, thus improving capital utilization efficiency. In terms of asset expansion, CCTP plans to expand to more assets beyond USDC, including EURC, USYC, and cirBTC, and support the issuance and circulation of real-world assets (RWA) across multiple chains. Furthermore, Circle is advancing cross-chain process coordination, simplifying multi-chain operation processes through tools such as Bridge Kit, Deposit Kit, Fee Service, and Workflows, achieving "one-click" cross-chain interaction, reducing development complexity, and improving user experience.

data.title: Tom Lee: The market may have bottomed out; I suggest focusing on assets that have led the market since the start of the war.
data.description: Bitmine Chairman Tom Lee stated on the X platform that although strong skepticism remains in the market, increasing signs indicate that the market has bottomed out. In an environment of high uncertainty, investors should focus on assets that have outperformed since the Iranian conflict, with Ethereum-related assets leading the pack, followed by Bitcoin-related assets. Specifically, Tom Lee prioritizes Ethereum and related assets (such as ETHA and BMNR), while also favoring Bitcoin and its ETFs (such as IBIT). He previously emphasized that Ethereum has demonstrated relative strength during geopolitical conflicts, becoming a "bellwether" for market resilience. Overall, Tom Lee believes that cryptocurrencies have proven to be a "wartime store of value," and the market has gradually digested negative factors, providing a foundation for further rebound in risk assets. However, this depends on the continued stability of the macroeconomic and geopolitical situation.

data.title: States Consider Fuel Tax Cuts Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: The ongoing conflict in Iran is leading states across the United States to contemplate temporary reductions in fuel taxes as a relief measure. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this initiative could potentially cost states millions of dollars. The move aims to alleviate the financial burden on consumers facing rising fuel prices due to the geopolitical tensions. However, the potential loss in tax revenue is a significant concern for state budgets already strained by other economic pressures. As discussions continue, states are weighing the benefits of immediate consumer relief against the long-term fiscal impacts.

data.title: Russia Bans Stanford University as 'Undesirable' Organization
data.description: Russia has officially designated Stanford University as an 'undesirable' organization, adding it to a growing list of foreign entities banned within the country. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this move is part of a broader crackdown on foreign institutions and organizations deemed to pose a threat to national security. The designation effectively prohibits the university from operating within Russia and restricts any collaboration with Russian entities. This action follows similar bans on other international organizations, reflecting ongoing tensions between Russia and Western institutions.

data.title: Significant NOM Transfer to Centralized Exchange Amid Price Drop
data.description:
On April 10, a substantial transfer of NOM tokens was observed. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, an address group holding at least 59% of NOM's circulating supply moved 23% of the tokens, totaling 674 million NOM, to a centralized exchange within the past hour. This transfer coincided with a 25% decline in NOM's price today.

The address group is likely responsible for driving a sixfold increase in NOM's value over the past two weeks, from $0.0017 to $0.0126. On April 1, they withdrew 1.72 billion NOM from a centralized exchange using seven wallets, representing 59% of NOM's total circulating supply of 2.9 billion tokens.

data.title: Iran Allegedly Charges Fees for Passage Through Hormuz Strait
data.description: An Israeli intelligence official has claimed that Iran is imposing fees on vessels passing through the Hormuz Strait. According to Odaily, the strait is reportedly under the complete control of the Iranian Revolutionary Guard, which determines which ships are allowed to pass and, more critically, which are not.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin surged to $73,000 after the CPI data release, with the market focusing on whether it can retest higher levels.
data.description: Bitcoin briefly rose to $73,000 after the release of the US March CPI data, attempting to set a new high for the period. Data showed that overall US inflation was slightly lower than market expectations, but a significant rise in energy prices created a divergence. Specifically, the US March CPI rose 3.3% year-on-year, and the month-on-month figure was 0.1% lower than expected; energy prices rose 10.9%, with gasoline prices surging 21.2% month-on-month, marking the largest monthly increase since 1967. Financial markets reacted relatively restrainedly, with US stocks opening largely flat and Bitcoin showing no significant volatility. Meanwhile, influenced by the previous PCE data, the market has largely ruled out the possibility of a short-term interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve. From a trading perspective, analysts believe that Bitcoin is currently trading within a converging range. If it retests key highs in the short term, market reaction will be a crucial signal for its subsequent trend, with the $74,000 level considered a key liquidity area. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Israeli officials say Iran is collecting tolls on passage through the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: An Israeli intelligence official stated that Iran is charging fees to ships allowed to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, which is effectively under the complete control of the Iranian Revolutionary Guard, who decides who can pass, but more importantly, who cannot. (Jin Shi)

data.title: U.S. President Trump Praises Palantir Technologies' Combat Capabilities
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has commended Palantir Technologies for its exceptional combat capabilities and equipment. According to Jin10, Trump emphasized the effectiveness of Palantir's technology in military operations, suggesting that adversaries are well aware of its prowess. Palantir Technologies, known for its data analytics and software solutions, has been increasingly involved in defense and intelligence sectors, providing tools that enhance operational efficiency and strategic decision-making. Trump's remarks highlight the growing importance of advanced technology in national security and defense strategies.

data.title: Optimism Enhances Wallet Execution Permissions on OP Mainnet
data.description: Optimism has introduced a new feature allowing agents and decentralized applications to request wallet execution permissions on the OP Mainnet. According to NS3.AI, this update enables MetaMask to let developers request these permissions using the ERC-7715 standard. This advancement provides Optimism applications with more detailed permission frameworks, moving beyond simple transaction approvals.

data.title: Palantir's Stock Decline Narrows to Under 2%
data.description: Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR.N) experienced a reduction in its stock decline, narrowing to less than 2% after previously falling by 6%. According to Jin10, the company's shares showed signs of recovery following earlier losses. The fluctuation in Palantir's stock price reflects ongoing market volatility and investor sentiment.

data.title: Today, US Bitcoin ETFs saw a net inflow of 4,614 BTC, and Ethereum ETFs saw a net inflow of 23,039 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, today the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net inflow of 4,614 BTC, the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 23,039 ETH, and the Solana ETF saw a net outflow of 12,345 SOL.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 73,000 USDT with a 3.35% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 10, 2026, 14:25 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 73,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 73,073.257813 USDT, with a narrowed 3.35% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Over $43 million in liquidations occurred across the entire network in the past hour, with $21.52 million in BTC liquidations.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, the total liquidation amount across the network in the past hour was $43.04 million, of which $38.65 million were short positions and $4.38 million were long positions. In addition, the liquidation amount of BTC reached $21.52 million and the liquidation amount of ETH reached $10.83 million.

data.title: OpenAI will be regulated by the EU's Digital Services Law and classified as a supermassive online search engine.
data.description: Sources say OpenAI will be regulated under the EU's Digital Services Act (DSA) and classified as a super-large online search engine. The European Commission plans to impose stricter regulations on ChatGPT in the future. (Jinshi)

data.title: The Bank of France is calling for tighter restrictions on MiCA stablecoin payments and plans to strengthen oversight of self-custodied wallets.
data.description: French regulators are strengthening the regulation of crypto assets from multiple directions. Denis Beau, Deputy Governor of the Bank of France, called for stricter restrictions on non-euro stablecoin payments under the EU's Crypto Asset Market Regulation (MiCA) framework, particularly for stablecoins pegged to the US dollar. Beau stated that the current MiCA does not adequately cover the risks associated with the large-scale adoption of non-European stablecoins and that regulatory measures should be further strengthened to address the dominance of US dollar stablecoins, which hold approximately 98% of the market share. Meanwhile, on April 7, the French National Assembly passed an anti-fraud bill requiring users of self-custodied crypto wallets holding more than €5,000 to submit annual reports. However, the bill has not yet completed all legislative procedures and remains controversial regarding its feasibility and data security. Overall, as Europe assesses the impact of US dollar stablecoins and advances the digital euro process, France is simultaneously tightening its regulation of on-chain assets and payment systems. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Tether's USDT Adoption Surges on Hyperliquid
data.description: On April 10, Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino announced that the adoption of USDT on Hyperliquid is rapidly increasing. According to BlockBeats, data reveals that USDT's share on Hyperliquid has grown significantly in less than three months, now accounting for 7.8% of the total trading volume on HIP-3.

data.title: Switzerland Seeks to Finalize U.S. Trade Deal by July
data.description: Switzerland is working towards finalizing a trade agreement with the United States by the end of July. Bloomberg posted on X that the European nation is seeking assurances that it will not face higher duties compared to rival countries. The Swiss government is keen to secure favorable terms in the deal, which is part of its broader strategy to strengthen economic ties with the U.S. The negotiations are ongoing, and both parties are expected to continue discussions to reach a mutually beneficial agreement.
